input.prompt:
I need to create a C# MCP server that uses HTTP transport for deployment
as a web service. It should expose both tools and resources (for example,
a resource that returns configuration data). Show me how to set up the
HTTP transport with MapMcp and implement a resource.

arguments.name: explore-mcp-api
arguments.prompt:
Explore the ModelContextProtocol and ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore NuGet packages installed in /tmp/sv-4715ce2cc4db4e12a3417bd6002b9fba/McpHttpServer to understand the API surface. Specifically I need to know:

1. How to register MCP services with DI (look for AddMcpServer or similar extension methods)
2. How to map the MCP HTTP endpoint (look for MapMcp or similar)
3. How to define resources (look for McpServerResource attribute, or resource registration patterns)
4. How to define tools (look for McpServerTool attribute)
5. Any relevant interfaces or base classes for tools/resources

Check the NuGet package DLLs in the ~/.nuget/packages/modelcontextprotocol/ and ~/.nuget/packages/modelcontextprotocol.aspnetcore/ directories. Use `dotnet tool` or decompile/list types if possible. Also check the obj/project.assets.json for dependency info.

Run commands like:
- Find the DLL paths in ~/.nuget/packages/
- Use `dotnet-ildasm` or `strings` on the DLLs to find type names and method signatures
- Check any README or xml doc files bundled with the packages

1. <?xml version="1.0"?>
2. <doc>
3.     <assembly>
4.         <name>ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore</name>
5.     </assembly>
6.     <members>
7.         <member name="T:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.HttpMcpServerBuilderExtensions">
8.             <summary>
9.             Provides methods for configuring HTTP MCP servers via dependency injection.
10.             </summary>
11.         </member>
12.         <member name="M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.HttpMcpServerBuilderExtensions.WithHttpTransport(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,System.Action{ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.HttpServerTransportOptions})">
13.             <summary>
14.             Adds the services necessary for <see cref="M:McpEndpointRouteBuilderExtensions.MapMcp"/>
15.             to handle MCP requests and sessions using the MCP Streamable HTTP transport. For more information on configuring the underlying HTTP server
16.             to control things like port binding custom TLS certificates, see the <see href="https://learn.microsoft.com/aspnet/core/fundamentals/minimal-apis">Minimal APIs quick reference</see>.
17.             </summary>
18.             <param name="builder">The builder instance.</param>
19.             <param name="configureOptions">Configures options for the Streamable HTTP transport. This allows configuring per-session
20.             <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerOptions"/> and running logic before and after a session.</param>
21.             <returns>The builder provided in <paramref name="builder"/>.</returns>
22.             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="builder"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
23.         </member>
24.         <member name="T:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.McpEndpointRouteBuilderExtensions">
25.             <summary>
26.             Provides extension methods for <see cref="T:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Routing.IEndpointRouteBuilder"/> to add MCP endpoints.
27.             </summary>
28.         </member>
29.         <member name="M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.McpEndpointRouteBuilderExtensions.MapMcp(Microsoft.AspNetCore.Routing.IEndpointRouteBuilder,System.String)">
30.             <summary>
31.             Sets up endpoints for handling MCP Streamable HTTP transport.
32.             See <see href="https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-03-26/basic/transports#streamable-http">the 2025-03-26 protocol specification</see> for details about the Streamable HTTP transport.
33.             Also maps legacy SSE endpoints for backward compatibility at the path "/sse" and "/message". <see href="https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2024-11-05/basic/transports#http-with-sse">the 2024-11-05 protocol specification</see> for details about the HTTP with SSE transport.
34.             </summary>
35.             <param name="endpoints">The web application to attach MCP HTTP endpoints.</param>
36.             <param name="pattern">The route pattern prefix to map to.</param>
37.             <returns>Returns a builder for configuring additional endpoint conventions like authorization policies.</returns>
38.         </member>
39.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.HttpServerTransportOptions">
40.             <summary>
41.             Configuration options for <see cref="M:McpEndpointRouteBuilderExtensions.MapMcp"/>.
42.             which implements the Streaming HTTP transport for the Model Context Protocol.
43.             See the protocol specification for details on the Streamable HTTP transport. <see href="https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-03-26/basic/transports#streamable-http"/>
44.             </summary>
45.         </member>
46.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.HttpServerTransportOptions.ConfigureSessionOptions">
47.             <summary>
48.             Gets or sets an optional asynchronous callback to configure per-session <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerOptions"/>
49.             with access to the <see cref="T:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Http.HttpContext"/> of the request that initiated the session.
50.             </summary>
51.         </member>
52.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.HttpServerTransportOptions.RunSessionHandler">
53.             <summary>
54.             Gets or sets an optional asynchronous callback for running new MCP sessions manually.
55.             This is useful for running logic before a sessions starts and after it completes.
56.             </summary>
57.         </member>
58.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.HttpServerTransportOptions.Stateless">
59.             <summary>
60.             Gets or sets whether the server should run in a stateless mode that does not require all requests for a given session
61.             to arrive to the same ASP.NET Core application process.
62.             </summary>
63.             <remarks>
64.             If <see langword="true"/>, the "/sse" endpoint will be disabled, and client information will be round-tripped as part
65.             of the "mcp-session-id" header instead of stored in memory. Unsolicited server-to-client messages and all server-to-client
66.             requests are also unsupported, because any responses may arrive at another ASP.NET Core application process.
67.             Client sampling and roots capabilities are also disabled in stateless mode, because the server cannot make requests.
68.             Defaults to <see langword="false"/>.
69.             </remarks>
70.         </member>
71.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.HttpServerTransportOptions.IdleTimeout">
72.             <summary>
73.             Gets or sets the duration of time the server will wait between any active requests before timing out an MCP session.
74.             </summary>
75.             <remarks>
76.             This is checked in background every 5 seconds. A client trying to resume a session will receive a 404 status code
77.             and should restart their session. A client can keep their session open by keeping a GET request open.
78.             Defaults to 2 hours.
79.             </remarks>
80.         </member>
81.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.HttpServerTransportOptions.MaxIdleSessionCount">
82.             <summary>
83.             Gets or sets maximum number of idle sessions to track in memory. This is used to limit the number of sessions that can be idle at once.
84.             </summary>
85.             <remarks>
86.             Past this limit, the server will log a critical error and terminate the oldest idle sessions even if they have not reached
87.             their <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.HttpServerTransportOptions.IdleTimeout"/> until the idle session count is below this limit. Clients that keep their session open by
88.             keeping a GET request open will not count towards this limit.
89.             Defaults to 100,000 sessions.
90.             </remarks>
91.         </member>
92.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.HttpServerTransportOptions.TimeProvider">
93.             <summary>
94.             Used for testing the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.HttpServerTransportOptions.IdleTimeout"/>.

 <?xml version="1.0"?>

     <members>
         <member name="T:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.HttpMcpServerBuilderExtensions">
             <summary>
             Provides methods for configuring HTTP MCP servers via dependency injection.
             </summary>
         </member>
         <member name="M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.HttpMcpServerBuilderExtensions.WithHttpTransport(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,System.Action{ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.HttpServerTransportOptions})">
             <summary>
             Adds the services necessary for <see cref="M:McpEndpointRouteBuilderExtensions.MapMcp"/>
             to handle MCP requests and sessions using the MCP Streamable HTTP transport. For more information on configuring the underlying HTTP server
             to control things like port binding custom TLS certificates, see the <see href="https://learn.microsoft.com/aspnet/core/fundamentals/minimal-apis">Minimal APIs quick reference</see>.
             </summary>
             <param name="builder">The builder instance.</param>
             <param name="configureOptions">Configures options for the Streamable HTTP transport. This allows configuring per-session
             <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerOptions"/> and running logic before and after a session.</param>
             <returns>The builder provided in <paramref name="builder"/>.</returns>
             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="builder"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
         </member>
         <member name="T:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.McpEndpointRouteBuilderExtensions">
             <summary>
             Provides extension methods for <see cref="T:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Routing.IEndpointRouteBuilder"/> to add MCP endpoints.
             </summary>
         </member>
         <member name="M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.McpEndpointRouteBuilderExtensions.MapMcp(Microsoft.AspNetCore.Routing.IEndpointRouteBuilder,System.String)">
             <summary>
             Sets up endpoints for handling MCP Streamable HTTP transport.
             See <see href="https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-03-26/basic/transports#streamable-http">the 2025-03-26 protocol specification</see> for details about the Streamable HTTP transport.
             Also maps legacy SSE endpoints for backward compatibility at the path "/sse" and "/message". <see href="https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2024-11-05/basic/transports#http-with-sse">the 2024-11-05 protocol specification</see> for details about the HTTP with SSE transport.
             </summary>
             <param name="endpoints">The web application to attach MCP HTTP endpoints.</param>
             <param name="pattern">The route pattern prefix to map to.</param>
             <returns>Returns a builder for configuring additional endpoint conventions like authorization policies.</returns>
         </member>
         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.HttpServerTransportOptions">
             <summary>
             Configuration options for <see cref="M:McpEndpointRouteBuilderExtensions.MapMcp"/>.
             which implements the Streaming HTTP transport for the Model Context Protocol.
             See the protocol specification for details on the Streamable HTTP transport. <see href="https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-03-26/basic/transports#streamable-http"/>
             </summary>
         </member>
         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.HttpServerTransportOptions.ConfigureSessionOptions">
             <summary>
             Gets or sets an optional asynchronous callback to configure per-session <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerOptions"/>
             with access to the <see cref="T:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Http.HttpContext"/> of the request that initiated the session.
             </summary>
         </member>
         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.HttpServerTransportOptions.RunSessionHandler">
             <summary>
             Gets or sets an optional asynchronous callback for running new MCP sessions manually.
             This is useful for running logic before a sessions starts and after it completes.
             </summary>
         </member>
         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.HttpServerTransportOptions.Stateless">
             <summary>
             Gets or sets whether the server should run in a stateless mode that does not require all requests for a given session
             to arrive to the same ASP.NET Core application process.
             </summary>
             <remarks>
             If <see langword="true"/>, the "/sse" endpoint will be disabled, and client information will be round-tripped as part
             of the "mcp-session-id" header instead of stored in memory. Unsolicited server-to-client messages and all server-to-client
             requests are also unsupported, because any responses may arrive at another ASP.NET Core application process.
             Client sampling and roots capabilities are also disabled in stateless mode, because the server cannot make requests.
             Defaults to <see langword="false"/>.
             </remarks>
         </member>
         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.HttpServerTransportOptions.IdleTimeout">
             <summary>
             Gets or sets the duration of time the server will wait between any active requests before timing out an MCP session.
             </summary>
             <remarks>
             This is checked in background every 5 seconds. A client trying to resume a session will receive a 404 status code
             and should restart their session. A client can keep their session open by keeping a GET request open.
             Defaults to 2 hours.
             </remarks>
         </member>
         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.HttpServerTransportOptions.MaxIdleSessionCount">
             <summary>
             Gets or sets maximum number of idle sessions to track in memory. This is used to limit the number of sessions that can be idle at once.
             </summary>
             <remarks>
             Past this limit, the server will log a critical error and terminate the oldest idle sessions even if they have not reached
             their <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.HttpServerTransportOptions.IdleTimeout"/> until the idle session count is below this limit. Clients that keep their session open by
             keeping a GET request open will not count towards this limit.
             Defaults to 100,000 sessions.
             </remarks>
         </member>
         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.HttpServerTransportOptions.TimeProvider">
             <summary>
             Used for testing the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.HttpServerTransportOptions.IdleTimeout"/>.
             </summary>
         </member>

        <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerTool">
            <summary>
            Represents an invocable tool used by Model Context Protocol clients and servers.
            </summary>
            <remarks>
            <para>
            <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerTool"/> is an abstract base class that represents an MCP tool for use in the server (as opposed
            to <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Tool"/>, which provides the protocol representation of a tool, and <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientTool"/>, which
            provides a client-side representation of a tool). Instances of <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerTool"/> can be added into a
            <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceCollection"/> to be picked up automatically when <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerFactory"/> is used to create
            an <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.IMcpServer"/>, or added into a <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPrimitiveCollection`1"/>.
            </para>
            <para>
            Most commonly, <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerTool"/> instances are created using the static <see cref="M:McpServerTool.Create"/> methods.
            These methods enable creating an <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerTool"/> for a method, specified via a <see cref="T:System.Delegate"/> or 
            <see cref="T:System.Reflection.MethodInfo"/>, and are what are used implicitly by WithToolsFromAssembly and WithTools. The <see cref="M:McpServerTool.Create"/> methods
            create <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerTool"/> instances capable of working with a large variety of .NET method signatures, automatically handling
            how parameters are marshaled into the method from the JSON received from the MCP client, and how the return value is marshaled back
            into the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolResponse"/> that's then serialized and sent back to the client.
            </para>
            <para>
            By default, parameters are sourced from the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolRequestParams.Arguments"/> dictionary, which is a collection
            of key/value pairs, and are represented in the JSON schema for the function, as exposed in the returned <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerTool"/>'s
            <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerTool.ProtocolTool"/>'s <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Tool.InputSchema"/>. Those parameters are deserialized from the
            <see cref="T:System.Text.Json.JsonElement"/> values in that collection. There are a few exceptions to this:
            <list type="bullet">
<exited with exit code 0>

<member name="M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithTools``1(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions)">
            <summary>Adds <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerTool"/> instances to the service collection backing <paramref name="builder"/>.</summary>
            <typeparam name="TToolType">The tool type.</typeparam>
            <param name="builder">The builder instance.</param>
            <param name="serializerOptions">The serializer options governing tool parameter marshalling.</param>
            <returns>The builder provided in <paramref name="builder"/>.</returns>
            <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="builder"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
            <remarks>
            This method discovers all instance and static methods (public and non-public) on the specified <typeparamref name="TToolType"/>
            type, where the methods are attributed as <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolAttribute"/>, and adds an <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerTool"/>
            instance for each. For instance methods, an instance is constructed for each invocation of the tool.
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithTools``1(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,``0,System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions)">
            <summary>Adds <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerTool"/> instances to the service collection backing <paramref name="builder"/>.</summary>
            <typeparam name="TToolType">The tool type.</typeparam>
            <param name="builder">The builder instance.</param>
            <param name="target">The target instance from which the tools should be sourced.</param>
            <param name="serializerOptions">The serializer options governing tool parameter marshalling.</param>
            <returns>The builder provided in <paramref name="builder"/>.</returns>
            <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="builder"/> or <paramref name="target"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
            <remarks>
            <para>
            This method discovers all methods (public and non-public) on the specified <typeparamref name="TToolType"/>
            type, where the methods are attributed as <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolAttribute"/>, and adds an <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerTool"/>
            instance for each, using <paramref name="target"/> as the associated instance for instance methods.
            </para>
            <para>
            However, if <typeparamref name="TToolType"/> is itself an <see cref="T:System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable`1"/> of <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerTool"/>,
            this method registers those tools directly without scanning for methods on <typeparamref name="TToolType"/>.
            </para>
            </remarks>
        </member>

        <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolAttribute">
            <summary>
            Used to indicate that a method should be considered an <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerTool"/>.
            </summary>
            <remarks>
            <para>
            This attribute is applied to methods that should be exposed as tools in the Model Context Protocol. When a class 
            containing methods marked with this attribute is registered with McpServerBuilderExtensions,
            these methods become available as tools that can be called by MCP clients.
            </para>
            <para>
            When methods are provided directly to <see cref="M:McpServerTool.Create"/>, the attribute is not required.
            </para>
            <para>
            By default, parameters are sourced from the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolRequestParams.Arguments"/> dictionary, which is a collection
            of key/value pairs, and are represented in the JSON schema for the function, as exposed in the returned <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerTool"/>'s

        <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerResourceAttribute">
            <summary>
            Used to indicate that a method or property should be considered an <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerResource"/>.
            </summary>
            <remarks>
            <para>
            This attribute is applied to methods or properties that should be exposed as resources in the Model Context Protocol. When a class 
            containing methods marked with this attribute is registered with McpServerBuilderExtensions,
            these methods or properties become available as resources that can be called by MCP clients.
            </para>
            <para>
            When methods are provided directly to <see cref="M:McpServerResource.Create"/>, the attribute is not required.
            </para>
            <para>
            Read resource requests do not contain separate arguments, only a URI. However, for templated resources, portions of that URI may be considered
            as arguments and may be bound to parameters. Further, resource methods may accept parameters that will be bound to arguments based on their type.

        <member name="M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.McpEndpointRouteBuilderExtensions.MapMcp(Microsoft.AspNetCore.Routing.IEndpointRouteBuilder,System.String)">
            <summary>
            Sets up endpoints for handling MCP Streamable HTTP transport.
            See <see href="https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-03-26/basic/transports#streamable-http">the 2025-03-26 protocol specification</see> for details about the Streamable HTTP transport.
            Also maps legacy SSE endpoints for backward compatibility at the path "/sse" and "/message". <see href="https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2024-11-05/basic/transports#http-with-sse">the 2024-11-05 protocol specification</see> for details about the HTTP with SSE transport.
            </summary>
            <param name="endpoints">The web application to attach MCP HTTP endpoints.</param>
            <param name="pattern">The route pattern prefix to map to.</param>
            <returns>Returns a builder for configuring additional endpoint conventions like authorization policies.</returns>
        </member>

<member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerResource.ProtocolResourceTemplate">
            <summary>Gets the protocol <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ResourceTemplate"/> type for this instance.</summary>
            <remarks>
            <para>
            The <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerResource.ProtocolResourceTemplate"/> property represents the underlying resource template definition as defined in the
            Model Context Protocol specification. It contains metadata like the resource templates's URI template, name, and description.
            </para>
            <para>
            Every valid resource URI is a valid resource URI template, and thus this property always returns an instance.
            In contrast, the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerResource.ProtocolResource"/> property may return <see langword="null"/> if the resource template
            contains a parameter, in which case the resource template URI is not a valid resource URI.
            </para>
            </remarks>
        </member>

            <summary>

            </summary>
            <remarks>
            <para>
            These capabilities are established during the initialization handshake and indicate
            which features the client supports, such as sampling, roots, and other
            protocol-specific functionality.
            </para>
            <para>
            Server implementations can check these capabilities to determine which features
            are available when interacting with the client.
            </para>

result.content:
<member name="T:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder">
            <summary>
            Provides a builder for configuring <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServer"/> instances.
            </summary>
            <remarks>
            <para>
            The <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder"/> interface provides a fluent API for configuring Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers
            when using dependency injection. It exposes methods for registering tools, prompts, custom request handlers,
            and server transports, allowing for comprehensive server configuration through a chain of method calls.
            </para>
            <para>
            The builder is obtained from the <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerServiceCollectionExtensions.AddMcpServer(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceCollection,System.Action{ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerOptions})"/> extension
            method and provides access to the underlying service collection via the <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder.Services"/> property.
            </para>
            </remarks>
        </member>
<exited with exit code 0>

            Sets up endpoints for handling MCP Streamable HTTP transport.
            </summary>
            <param name="endpoints">The web application to attach MCP HTTP endpoints.</param>
            <param name="pattern">The route pattern prefix to map to.</param>
            <returns>Returns a builder for configuring additional endpoint conventions like authorization policies.</returns>
            <exception cref="T:System.InvalidOperationException">The required MCP services have not been registered. Ensure <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.HttpMcpServerBuilderExtensions.WithHttpTransport(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,System.Action{ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.HttpServerTransportOptions})"/> has been called during application startup.</exception>
            <remarks>
            For details about the Streamable HTTP transport, see the <see href="https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-11-25/basic/transports#streamable-http">2025-11-25 protocol specification</see>.
            When legacy SSE is enabled via <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.HttpServerTransportOptions.EnableLegacySse"/>, this method also maps legacy SSE endpoints at the path "/sse" and "/message". For details about the HTTP with SSE transport, see the <see href="https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2024-11-05/basic/transports#http-with-sse">2024-11-05 protocol specification</see>.
            </remarks>

data.content: Excellent! Now I have comprehensive information. Let me create a summary document with all the key findings:

arguments.command:
cat > /tmp/mcp_api_summary.txt << 'EOF'
# ModelContextProtocol API Surface Summary

## Packages Used
- **ModelContextProtocol**: v1.2.0 (Core server implementation)
- **ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore**: v1.2.0 (ASP.NET Core integration)

## 1. DI REGISTRATION - AddMcpServer Extension Method

**Location**: `Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerServiceCollectionExtensions`

**Extension Method**:
```csharp
public static IMcpServerBuilder AddMcpServer(
    this IServiceCollection services,
    Action<ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerOptions> configureOptions = null)
```

**Usage**:
- Adds the Model Context Protocol (MCP) server to the service collection with default options
- Accepts optional callback to configure `McpServerOptions`
- Returns `IMcpServerBuilder` for fluent configuration
- Automatically picks up tools, resources, and prompts registered in DI as `IEnumerable<McpServerTool>`, `IEnumerable<McpServerResource>`, and `IEnumerable<McpServerPrompt>`

**Related Type**: `IMcpServerBuilder`
- Fluent API for configuring MCP servers
- Provides access to underlying service collection via `Services` property
- Has method chains like `.WithHttpTransport()`, `.WithTools()`, `.WithResources()`, etc.

---

## 2. HTTP ENDPOINT MAPPING - MapMcp Extension Method

**Location**: `Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.McpEndpointRouteBuilderExtensions`

**Extension Method**:
```csharp
public static IEndpointConventionBuilder MapMcp(
    this IEndpointRouteBuilder endpoints,
    string pattern)
```

**Usage**:
- Sets up endpoints for handling MCP Streamable HTTP transport
- Maps to the specified route pattern prefix
- Also maps legacy SSE endpoints at "/sse" and "/message" when enabled
- Returns builder for configuring additional endpoint conventions (e.g., authorization policies)

**Prerequisites**:
- Requires `WithHttpTransport()` to be called during application startup
- Throws `InvalidOperationException` if required MCP services not registered

**Protocol Support**:
- Streamable HTTP transport (2025-11-25 spec): Primary transport
- Legacy HTTP with SSE transport (2024-11-05 spec): Backward compatibility

---

## 3. HTTP TRANSPORT SETUP - WithHttpTransport Extension Method

**Location**: `Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.HttpMcpServerBuilderExtensions`

**Extension Method**:
```csharp
public static IMcpServerBuilder WithHttpTransport(
    this IMcpServerBuilder builder,
    Action<ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.HttpServerTransportOptions> configureOptions)
```

**Purpose**:
- Adds services necessary for `MapMcp()` to handle MCP requests and sessions
- Configures Streamable HTTP transport options

**HttpServerTransportOptions Properties**:
- `ConfigureSessionOptions`: Callback to configure per-session `McpServerOptions` with access to `HttpContext`
- `RunSessionHandler`: Callback for running new MCP sessions manually (pre-start and post-completion logic)
- `Stateless`: Whether server runs in stateless mode (sessions don't require same ASP.NET process)
- `IdleTimeout`: Duration to wait between requests before timing out session (default: 2 hours)
- `MaxIdleSessionCount`: Maximum idle sessions to track in memory (default: 100,000)
- `EnableLegacySse`: Whether to enable legacy SSE endpoints (controlled separately)
- `TimeProvider`: For testing timeout behavior

---

## 4. TOOL REGISTRATION - McpServerTool & McpServerToolAttribute

### McpServerTool Base Class

**Namespace**: `ModelContextProtocol.Server`

**Abstract Class**:
```csharp
public abstract class McpServerTool
```

**Key Members**:
- `Property ProtocolTool`: Gets the protocol `Tool` type for this instance
- `Abstract Method InvokeAsync(RequestContext<CallToolRequestParams>, CancellationToken)`: 
  - Returns `ValueTask<CallToolResponse>`
  - Invokes the tool with request parameters

**Static Factory Methods** (`McpServerTool.Create()`):
1. From Delegate: `Create(Delegate method, McpServerToolCreateOptions options)`
2. From MethodInfo with instance: `Create(MethodInfo method, object instance, McpServerToolCreateOptions options)`
3. From MethodInfo with factory: `Create(MethodInfo method, Func<RequestContext<CallToolRequestParams>, object> factory, McpServerToolCreateOptions options)`
4. From AIFunction: `Create(AIFunction aiFunction, McpServerToolCreateOptions options)`

### McpServerToolAttribute

**Purpose**: Indicates that a method should be exposed as an `McpServerTool`

**Applied to**: Methods in classes

**Automatic Discovery**:
- Used by `.WithTools<TToolType>()` and `.WithToolsFromAssembly()` to discover tool methods
- Not required when creating tools manually via `McpServerTool.Create()`

**Parameter Binding**:
- Parameters sourced from `CallToolRequestParams.Arguments` dictionary (key/value pairs)
- Automatically deserialized from JSON to method parameters
- Represented in JSON schema exposed via `Tool.InputSchema`

---

## 5. RESOURCE REGISTRATION - McpServerResource & McpServerResourceAttribute

### McpServerResource Base Class

**Namespace**: `ModelContextProtocol.Server`

**Abstract Class**:
```csharp
public abstract class McpServerResource
```

**Key Members**:
- `Property ProtocolResourceTemplate`: Gets the protocol `ResourceTemplate` type (always returns instance)
- `Property ProtocolResource`: Gets the protocol `Resource` type (may be null for templated resources)
- `Abstract Method ReadAsync(RequestContext<ReadResourceRequestParams>, CancellationToken)`:
  - Returns `ValueTask<ReadResourceResult?>`
  - Returns null if resource URI doesn't match
  - Reads/renders resource with provided parameters

**Resource Types**:
- Direct resources: e.g., `resource://example`
- Templated resources: e.g., `resource://example/{id}`

**Static Factory Methods** (`McpServerResource.Create()`):
1. From Delegate: `Create(Delegate method, McpServerResourceCreateOptions options)`
2. From MethodInfo with instance: `Create(MethodInfo method, object instance, McpServerResourceCreateOptions options)`
3. From MethodInfo with factory: `Create(MethodInfo method, Func<RequestContext<ReadResourceRequestParams>, object> factory, McpServerResourceCreateOptions options)`
4. From AIFunction: `Create(AIFunction aiFunction, McpServerResourceCreateOptions options)`

### McpServerResourceAttribute

**Purpose**: Indicates that a method or property should be exposed as an `McpServerResource`

**Applied to**: Methods or properties in classes

**Automatic Discovery**:
- Used by `.WithResources<TResourceType>()` and `.WithResourcesFromAssembly()` to discover resource methods
- Not required when creating resources manually via `McpServerResource.Create()`

**Parameter Binding**:
- Parameters bound from URI (including templated portions)
- Resources may also accept parameters of certain types that bind to arguments
- No separate arguments dictionary like tools

---

## 6. BUILDER EXTENSION METHODS FOR CONFIGURATION

### WithTools Methods

**Generic Registration**:
- `builder.WithTools<TToolType>(JsonSerializerOptions?)`: Discovers `[McpServerTool]` methods in `TToolType`
- `builder.WithTools<TToolType>(TToolType instance, JsonSerializerOptions?)`: Uses provided instance
- `builder.WithTools(IEnumerable<Type> toolTypes, JsonSerializerOptions?)`: Multiple types
- `builder.WithToolsFromAssembly(Assembly?, JsonSerializerOptions?)`: Types marked with `[McpServerToolTypeAttribute]`

**Direct Registration**:
- `builder.WithTools(IEnumerable<McpServerTool> tools)`: Register pre-created tool instances directly

### WithResources Methods

**Generic Registration**:
- `builder.WithResources<TResourceType>()`: Discovers `[McpServerResource]` methods in `TResourceType`
- `builder.WithResources<TResourceType>(TResourceType instance)`: Uses provided instance
- `builder.WithResources(IEnumerable<Type> resourceTypes)`: Multiple types
- `builder.WithResourcesFromAssembly(Assembly?)`: Types marked with `[McpServerResourceTypeAttribute]`

**Direct Registration**:
- `builder.WithResources(IEnumerable<McpServerResource> resourceTemplates)`: Register pre-created instances

### Other Builder Methods

- `.WithPrompts<TPromptType>(...)`: Register prompts (similar pattern to tools/resources)
- `.WithHttpTransport(Action<HttpServerTransportOptions>)`: Enable HTTP transport

## TYPICAL SETUP FLOW

1. **Create WebApplication**:
   ```csharp
   var builder = WebApplication.CreateBuilder(args);
   var app = builder.Build();
   ```

2. **Register MCP Server**:
   ```csharp
   var mcp = builder.Services.AddMcpServer(options => 
   {
       // Configure server options if needed
   });
   ```

3. **Configure HTTP Transport**:
   ```csharp
   mcp.WithHttpTransport(options =>
   {
       options.ConfigureSessionOptions = async (context, serverOptions) =>
       {
           // Configure per-session options with access to HttpContext
       };
   });
   ```

4. **Register Tools**:
   ```csharp
   mcp.WithTools<MyToolsClass>();  // Auto-discovers [McpServerTool] methods
   ```

5. **Register Resources**:
   ```csharp
   mcp.WithResources<MyResourcesClass>();  // Auto-discovers [McpServerResource] methods
   ```

6. **Map HTTP Endpoint**:
   ```csharp
   app.MapMcp("/mcp");  // Maps MCP HTTP endpoints to /mcp
   ```

7. **Run Application**:
   ```csharp
   app.Run();
   ```

# ModelContextProtocol API Surface Summary

## Packages Used
- **ModelContextProtocol**: v1.2.0 (Core server implementation)
- **ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore**: v1.2.0 (ASP.NET Core integration)

## 1. DI REGISTRATION - AddMcpServer Extension Method

**Location**: `Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerServiceCollectionExtensions`

**Extension Method**:
```csharp
public static IMcpServerBuilder AddMcpServer(
    this IServiceCollection services,
    Action<ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerOptions> configureOptions = null)
```

**Usage**:
- Adds the Model Context Protocol (MCP) server to the service collection with default options
- Accepts optional callback to configure `McpServerOptions`
- Returns `IMcpServerBuilder` for fluent configuration
- Automatically picks up tools, resources, and prompts registered in DI as `IEnumerable<McpServerTool>`, `IEnumerable<McpServerResource>`, and `IEnumerable<McpServerPrompt>`

**Related Type**: `IMcpServerBuilder`
- Fluent API for configuring MCP servers
- Provides access to underlying service collection via `Services` property
- Has method chains like `.WithHttpTransport()`, `.WithTools()`, `.WithResources()`, etc.

---

## 2. HTTP ENDPOINT MAPPING - MapMcp Extension Method

**Location**: `Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.McpEndpointRouteBuilderExtensions`

**Extension Method**:
```csharp
public static IEndpointConventionBuilder MapMcp(
    this IEndpointRouteBuilder endpoints,
    string pattern)
```

**Usage**:
- Sets up endpoints for handling MCP Streamable HTTP transport
- Maps to the specified route pattern prefix
- Also maps legacy SSE endpoints at "/sse" and "/message" when enabled
- Returns builder for configuring additional endpoint conventions (e.g., authorization policies)

**Prerequisites**:
- Requires `WithHttpTransport()` to be called during application startup
- Throws `InvalidOperationException` if required MCP services not registered

**Protocol Support**:
- Streamable HTTP transport (2025-11-25 spec): Primary transport
- Legacy HTTP with SSE transport (2024-11-05 spec): Backward compatibility

---

## 3. HTTP TRANSPORT SETUP - WithHttpTransport Extension Method

**Location**: `Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.HttpMcpServerBuilderExtensions`

**Extension Method**:
```csharp
public static IMcpServerBuilder WithHttpTransport(
    this IMcpServerBuilder builder,
    Action<ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.HttpServerTransportOptions> configureOptions)
```

**Purpose**:
- Adds services necessary for `MapMcp()` to handle MCP requests and sessions
- Configures Streamable HTTP transport options

**HttpServerTransportOptions Properties**:
- `ConfigureSessionOptions`: Callback to configure per-session `McpServerOptions` with access to `HttpContext`
- `RunSessionHandler`: Callback for running new MCP sessions manually (pre-start and post-completion logic)
- `Stateless`: Whether server runs in stateless mode (sessions don't require same ASP.NET process)
- `IdleTimeout`: Duration to wait between requests before timing out session (default: 2 hours)
- `MaxIdleSessionCount`: Maximum idle sessions to track in memory (default: 100,000)
- `EnableLegacySse`: Whether to enable legacy SSE endpoints (controlled separately)
- `TimeProvider`: For testing timeout behavior

---

## 4. TOOL REGISTRATION - McpServerTool & McpServerToolAttribute

### McpServerTool Base Class

**Namespace**: `ModelContextProtocol.Server`

**Abstract Class**:
```csharp
public abstract class McpServerTool
```

**Key Members**:
- `Property ProtocolTool`: Gets the protocol `Tool` type for this instance
- `Abstract Method InvokeAsync(RequestContext<CallToolRequestParams>, CancellationToken)`: 
  - Returns `ValueTask<CallToolResponse>`
  - Invokes the tool with request parameters

**Static Factory Methods** (`McpServerTool.Create()`):
1. From Delegate: `Create(Delegate method, McpServerToolCreateOptions options)`
2. From MethodInfo with instance: `Create(MethodInfo method, object instance, McpServerToolCreateOptions options)`
3. From MethodInfo with factory: `Create(MethodInfo method, Func<RequestContext<CallToolRequestParams>, object> factory, McpServerToolCreateOptions options)`
4. From AIFunction: `Create(AIFunction aiFunction, McpServerToolCreateOptions options)`

### McpServerToolAttribute

**Purpose**: Indicates that a method should be exposed as an `McpServerTool`

**Applied to**: Methods in classes

**Automatic Discovery**:
- Used by `.WithTools<TToolType>()` and `.WithToolsFromAssembly()` to discover tool methods
- Not required when creating tools manually via `McpServerTool.Create()`

**Parameter Binding**:
- Parameters sourced from `CallToolRequestParams.Arguments` dictionary (key/value pairs)
- Automatically deserialized from JSON to method parameters
- Represented in JSON schema exposed via `Tool.InputSchema`

---

## 5. RESOURCE REGISTRATION - McpServerResource & McpServerResourceAttribute

### McpServerResource Base Class

**Namespace**: `ModelContextProtocol.Server`

**Abstract Class**:
```csharp
public abstract class McpServerResource
```

**Key Members**:
- `Property ProtocolResourceTemplate`: Gets the protocol `ResourceTemplate` type (always returns instance)
- `Property ProtocolResource`: Gets the protocol `Resource` type (may be null for templated resources)
- `Abstract Method ReadAsync(RequestContext<ReadResourceRequestParams>, CancellationToken)`:
  - Returns `ValueTask<ReadResourceResult?>`
  - Returns null if resource URI doesn't match
  - Reads/renders resource with provided parameters

**Resource Types**:
- Direct resources: e.g., `resource://example`
- Templated resources: e.g., `resource://example/{id}`

**Static Factory Methods** (`McpServerResource.Create()`):
1. From Delegate: `Create(Delegate method, McpServerResourceCreateOptions options)`
2. From MethodInfo with instance: `Create(MethodInfo method, object instance, McpServerResourceCreateOptions options)`
3. From MethodInfo with factory: `Create(MethodInfo method, Func<RequestContext<ReadResourceRequestParams>, object> factory, McpServerResourceCreateOptions options)`
4. From AIFunction: `Create(AIFunction aiFunction, McpServerResourceCreateOptions options)`

### McpServerResourceAttribute

**Purpose**: Indicates that a method or property should be exposed as an `McpServerResource`

**Applied to**: Methods or properties in classes

**Automatic Discovery**:
- Used by `.WithResources<TResourceType>()` and `.WithResourcesFromAssembly()` to discover resource methods
- Not required when creating resources manually via `McpServerResource.Create()`

**Parameter Binding**:
- Parameters bound from URI (including templated portions)
- Resources may also accept parameters of certain types that bind to arguments
- No separate arguments dictionary like tools

data.content:
Perfect! Let me output this in a more readable format directly:

---

## Summary: ModelContextProtocol & ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore API Surface

I've analyzed the NuGet packages (**ModelContextProtocol v1.2.0** and **ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore v1.2.0**) from the XML documentation in your project. Here's the complete API surface:

### 1. **DI Service Registration: AddMcpServer**

**Namespace**: `Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerServiceCollectionExtensions`

```csharp
public static IMcpServerBuilder AddMcpServer(
    this IServiceCollection services,
    Action<ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerOptions> configureOptions = null)
```

- **Purpose**: Registers MCP server services with the service collection
- **Returns**: `IMcpServerBuilder` for fluent configuration
- **Auto-discovery**: Automatically picks up registered `IEnumerable<McpServerTool>`, `IEnumerable<McpServerResource>`, and `IEnumerable<McpServerPrompt>` from DI

---

### 2. **HTTP Endpoint Mapping: MapMcp**

**Namespace**: `Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.McpEndpointRouteBuilderExtensions`

```csharp
public static IEndpointConventionBuilder MapMcp(
    this IEndpointRouteBuilder endpoints,
    string pattern)
```

- **Purpose**: Sets up HTTP endpoints for MCP Streamable HTTP transport
- **Parameters**: `pattern` = route prefix (e.g., "/mcp")
- **Returns**: Builder for configuring authorization policies and other conventions
- **Supports**:
  - Streamable HTTP transport (primary, 2025-11-25 spec)
  - Legacy HTTP with SSE (backward compatibility, when enabled)
- **Exception**: Throws `InvalidOperationException` if `.WithHttpTransport()` not called

---

### 3. **HTTP Transport Configuration: WithHttpTransport**

**Namespace**: `Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.HttpMcpServerBuilderExtensions`

```csharp
public static IMcpServerBuilder WithHttpTransport(
    this IMcpServerBuilder builder,
    Action<HttpServerTransportOptions> configureOptions)
```

**HttpServerTransportOptions Properties**:
- `ConfigureSessionOptions`: `Func<RequestContext, ValueTask>` - Configure per-session with `HttpContext` access
- `RunSessionHandler`: `Func<IAsyncEnumerable<...>, ValueTask>` - Run/manage session lifecycle
- `Stateless`: `bool` - Stateless mode (no per-instance session storage)
- `IdleTimeout`: `TimeSpan` - Session idle timeout (default: 2 hours)
- `MaxIdleSessionCount`: `int` - Max idle sessions in memory (default: 100,000)
- `EnableLegacySse`: `bool` - Enable legacy SSE endpoints at "/sse" and "/message"
- `TimeProvider`: `TimeProvider` - For testing timeout behavior

---

### 4. **Tool Registration: McpServerToolAttribute & McpServerTool**

**Attribute Location**: `ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolAttribute`

```csharp
[AttributeUsage(AttributeTargets.Method)]
public class McpServerToolAttribute : Attribute { }
```

**Usage**: Mark methods that should be exposed as MCP tools

**McpServerTool Base Class** (`ModelContextProtocol.Server`):

```csharp
public abstract class McpServerTool
{
    public Tool ProtocolTool { get; }
    public abstract ValueTask<CallToolResponse> InvokeAsync(
        RequestContext<CallToolRequestParams> request,
        CancellationToken cancellationToken);
}
```

**Static Factory Methods** (`McpServerTool.Create()`):
1. From `Delegate`: `Create(Delegate method, McpServerToolCreateOptions)`
2. From `MethodInfo` + instance: `Create(MethodInfo, object, McpServerToolCreateOptions)`
3. From `MethodInfo` + factory: `Create(MethodInfo, Func<RequestContext, object>, McpServerToolCreateOptions)`
4. From `AIFunction`: `Create(AIFunction, McpServerToolCreateOptions)`

**Parameter Binding**: Parameters come from `CallToolRequestParams.Arguments` dictionary, deserialized to method parameters, represented in `Tool.InputSchema` as JSON schema

---

### 5. **Resource Registration: McpServerResourceAttribute & McpServerResource**

**Attribute Location**: `ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerResourceAttribute`

```csharp
[AttributeUsage(AttributeTargets.Method | AttributeTargets.Property)]
public class McpServerResourceAttribute : Attribute { }
```

**Usage**: Mark methods or properties that should be exposed as MCP resources

**McpServerResource Base Class** (`ModelContextProtocol.Server`):

```csharp
public abstract class McpServerResource
{
    public ResourceTemplate ProtocolResourceTemplate { get; }
    public Resource ProtocolResource { get; }  // null for templated resources
    public abstract ValueTask<ReadResourceResult?> ReadAsync(
        RequestContext<ReadResourceRequestParams> request,
        CancellationToken cancellationToken);
}
```

**Resource Types**:
- Direct: `resource://example`
- Templated: `resource://example/{id}`

**Static Factory Methods** (`McpServerResource.Create()`):
1. From `Delegate`: `Create(Delegate method, McpServerResourceCreateOptions)`
2. From `MethodInfo` + instance: `Create(MethodInfo, object, McpServerResourceCreateOptions)`
3. From `MethodInfo` + factory: `Create(MethodInfo, Func<RequestContext, object>, McpServerResourceCreateOptions)`
4. From `AIFunction`: `Create(AIFunction, McpServerResourceCreateOptions)`

**Parameter Binding**: Parameters bound from URI (including templated portions like `{id}`), not from separate arguments dict

---

### 6. **Builder Extension Methods**

**IMcpServerBuilder** (`Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection`):

**Tool Registration**:
- `WithTools<TToolType>(JsonSerializerOptions?)` - Auto-discover `[McpServerTool]` methods
- `WithTools<TToolType>(TToolType instance, JsonSerializerOptions?)` - Use provided instance
- `WithTools(IEnumerable<Type> toolTypes, JsonSerializerOptions?)` - Multiple types
- `WithToolsFromAssembly(Assembly?, JsonSerializerOptions?)` - Types with `[McpServerToolTypeAttribute]`
- `WithTools(IEnumerable<McpServerTool> tools)` - Register pre-created instances

**Resource Registration**:
- `WithResources<TResourceType>()` - Auto-discover `[McpServerResource]` methods
- `WithResources<TResourceType>(TResourceType instance)` - Use provided instance
- `WithResources(IEnumerable<Type> resourceTypes)` - Multiple types
- `WithResourcesFromAssembly(Assembly?)` - Types with `[McpServerResourceTypeAttribute]`
- `WithResources(IEnumerable<McpServerResource> resourceTemplates)` - Register pre-created instances

**Prompt Registration**:
- `WithPrompts<TPromptType>(...)` - Similar pattern to tools/resources

---

### 7. **Base Classes for Delegation**

**DelegatingMcpServerTool** & **DelegatingMcpServerResource**:
- Base classes for wrapping and chaining tools/resources
- Recommended for building decorators or middleware for tools/resources

**AIFunctionMcpServerTool** & **AIFunctionMcpServerResource**:
- Specialized implementations wrapping `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction`
- Automatically derive schema and URI templates from AIFunction metadata

---

### **Typical Setup Pattern**

```csharp
var builder = WebApplication.CreateBuilder(args);

// 1. Register MCP server
var mcp = builder.Services.AddMcpServer();

// 2. Configure HTTP transport
mcp.WithHttpTransport(options =>
{
    options.ConfigureSessionOptions = async (context, opts) => { };
});

// 3. Register tools (auto-discovers [McpServerTool] methods)
mcp.WithTools<MyToolsClass>();

// 4. Register resources (auto-discovers [McpServerResource] methods)
mcp.WithResources<MyResourcesClass>();

// 5. Map HTTP endpoints
var app = builder.Build();
app.MapMcp("/mcp");

app.Run();
```

toolResult.textResultForLlm: Validates that is set. Represents configuration options for , which implements the Streamable HTTP transport for the Model Context Protocol. See the protocol specification for details on the Streamable HTTP transport. For details on the Streamable HTTP transport, see the protocol specification. Gets or sets an optional asynchronous callback to configure per-session with access to the of the request that initiated the session. In stateful mode (the default), this callback is invoked once per session when the client sends the initialize request. In mode, it is invoked on every HTTP request because each request creates a fresh server context. Gets or sets an optional asynchronous callback for running new MCP sessions manually. This callback is useful for running logic before a session starts and after it completes. The parameter comes from the request that initiated the session (e.g., the initialize request) and may not be usable after starts, since that request will have already completed. -- Provides methods for configuring HTTP MCP servers via dependency injection. Adds the services necessary for to handle MCP requests and sessions using the MCP Streamable HTTP transport. The builder instance. Configures options for the Streamable HTTP transport. This allows configuring per-session and running logic before and after a session. The builder provided in . For more information on configuring the underlying HTTP server

Gets or sets an optional asynchronous callback to configure per-session with access to the of the request that initiated the session. In stateful mode (the default), this callback is invoked once per session when the client sends the initialize request. In mode, it is invoked on every HTTP request because each request creates a fresh server context. Gets or sets an optional asynchronous callback for running new MCP sessions manually. This callback is useful for running logic before a session starts and after it completes. The parameter comes from the request that initiated the session (e.g., the initialize request) and may not be usable after starts, since that request will have already completed. -- Provides methods for configuring HTTP MCP servers via dependency injection. Adds the services necessary for to handle MCP requests and sessions using the MCP Streamable HTTP transport. The builder instance. Configures options for the Streamable HTTP transport. This allows configuring per-session and running logic before and after a session. The builder provided in . For more information on configuring the underlying HTTP server

toolResult.textResultForLlm:
<member name="M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.McpEndpointRouteBuilderExtensions.MapMcp(Microsoft.AspNetCore.Routing.IEndpointRouteBuilder,System.String)">
            <summary>
            Sets up endpoints for handling MCP Streamable HTTP transport.
            </summary>
            <param name="endpoints">The web application to attach MCP HTTP endpoints.</param>
            <param name="pattern">The route pattern prefix to map to.</param>
            <returns>Returns a builder for configuring additional endpoint conventions like authorization policies.</returns>
            <exception cref="T:System.InvalidOperationException">The required MCP services have not been registered. Ensure <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.HttpMcpServerBuilderExtensions.WithHttpTransport(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,System.Action{ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.HttpServerTransportOptions})"/> has been called during application startup.</exception>
            <remarks>
            For details about the Streamable HTTP transport, see the <see href="https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-11-25/basic/transports#streamable-http">2025-11-25 protocol specification</see>.
            When legacy SSE is enabled via <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.HttpServerTransportOptions.EnableLegacySse"/>, this method also maps legacy SSE endpoints at the path "/sse" and "/message". For details about the HTTP with SSE transport, see the <see href="https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2024-11-05/basic/transports#http-with-sse">2024-11-05 protocol specification</see>.
            </remarks>
        </member>
    </members>
</doc>

arguments.command:
cd /tmp/sv-4715ce2cc4db4e12a3417bd6002b9fba/McpHttpServer && dotnet build --no-restore -q 2>&1 && echo "BUILD OK"
echo "---"
ASPNETCORE_URLS=http://localhost:5996 dotnet run --no-launch-profile &
BGPID=$!
sleep 4

# 1. Initialize
echo "=== INITIALIZE ==="
curl -s --max-time 5 -X POST http://localhost:5996/mcp \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Accept: application/json, text/event-stream" \
  -d '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2025-03-26","capabilities":{},"clientInfo":{"name":"test","version":"1.0"}}}' 2>&1
echo ""

# Extract session ID
SESSION_ID=$(curl -s --max-time 5 -X POST http://localhost:5996/mcp \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Accept: application/json, text/event-stream" \
  -D - -o /dev/null \
  -d '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2025-03-26","capabilities":{},"clientInfo":{"name":"test","version":"1.0"}}}' 2>/dev/null | grep -i 'mcp-session-id' | tr -d '\r' | awk '{print $2}')
echo "Session: $SESSION_ID"

# 2. List tools
echo "=== LIST TOOLS ==="
curl -s --max-time 5 -X POST http://localhost:5996/mcp \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Accept: application/json, text/event-stream" \
  -H "Mcp-Session-Id: $SESSION_ID" \
  -d '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":2,"method":"tools/list","params":{}}' 2>&1
echo ""

# 3. List resources
echo "=== LIST RESOURCES ==="
curl -s --max-time 5 -X POST http://localhost:5996/mcp \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Accept: application/json, text/event-stream" \
  -H "Mcp-Session-Id: $SESSION_ID" \
  -d '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":3,"method":"resources/list","params":{}}' 2>&1
echo ""

# 4. Read a resource
echo "=== READ RESOURCE ==="
curl -s --max-time 5 -X POST http://localhost:5996/mcp \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Accept: application/json, text/event-stream" \
  -H "Mcp-Session-Id: $SESSION_ID" \
  -d '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":4,"method":"resources/read","params":{"uri":"config://app"}}' 2>&1
echo ""

kill $BGPID 2>/dev/null; wait $BGPID 2>/dev/null
